title: தூரத்து தேசத்து விருந்தினர் (A Visitor from Distant Lands) - மாதிரித் தேர்வு
subject: english

questions.question: Which vegetable did Mani want to throw away? / மணி எதனைத் தூக்கி எறிய விரும்பினான்?
questions.explanation: Mani thought chilies were too spicy and didn't know they were 'visitors'.

questions.question: Where did chilies come from? / மிளகாய் எங்கிருந்து வந்தது?
questions.explanation: Chilies originated in South America and were later brought to the rest of the world.

questions.question: Who brought chilies to India? / மிளகாயை இந்தியாவிற்கு கொண்டு வந்தது யார்?
questions.explanation: Portuguese traders introduced chilies to the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which explorer 'discovered' America? / அமெரிக்காவைக் கண்டுபிடித்த மாலுமி யார்?
questions.explanation: Columbus reached America while looking for a sea route to India.

questions.question: Columbus was actually searching for ______. / கொலம்பஸ் உண்மையில் எதனைத் தேடிக்கொண்டிருந்தார்?
questions.explanation: He wanted to find India to get its famous black pepper.

questions.question: Black pepper was called ______ in ancient India. / பண்டைய இந்தியாவில் கருப்பு மிளகு எவ்வாறு அழைக்கப்பட்டது?
questions.explanation: Pepper was so valuable that it was known as Black Gold.

questions.question: Vasco da Gama sailed to ______. / வாஸ்கோடகாமா எங்கு வந்து சேர்ந்தார்?
questions.explanation: Vasco da Gama reaching Calicut (Kozhikode) in Kerala was a major historic event.

questions.question: Ladies' finger originated from ______. / வெண்டைக்காய் எங்கிருந்து வந்தது?
questions.explanation: Ladies' finger (Okra) is native to Africa.

questions.question: What was the curd rice eaten with? / தயிர் சாதம் எதனுடன் உண்ணப்பட்டது?
questions.explanation: The story mentions Mani was eating curd rice and potato curry.

questions.question: Corn and Tomato also came from ______. / சோளமும் தக்காளியும் எங்கிருந்து வந்தன?
questions.explanation: Like chilies and potatoes, corn and tomatoes are from South America.

questions.question: The word 'Delicious' means ______. / 'Delicious' என்ற சொல்லின் பொருள் ______.
questions.explanation: Delicious means having a very pleasing taste.

questions.question: Who scolded Mani for wasting food? / உணவை வீணடித்ததற்காக மணியைத் திட்டியது யார்?
questions.explanation: His sister Selvi told him off for about to waste the chilies.

questions.question: Guntur is a district in ______. / குண்டூர் எந்த மாநிலத்தில் உள்ள மாவட்டம்?
questions.explanation: Guntur in Andhra Pradesh is very famous for its chili production.

questions.question: Which spice was very expensive in the olden days? / பழைய காலத்தில் எந்த நறுமணப் பொருள் விலை உயர்ந்ததாக இருந்தது?
questions.explanation: Black pepper was the primary reason for European exploration.

questions.question: Columbus called chilies '______' because they were spicy.
questions.explanation: He compared their spiciness to black pepper and called them 'red pepper'.

questions.question: Mani's family thought the 'Visitor' was a ______. / மணியின் குடும்பம் 'விருந்தினர்' என்பது ஒரு ______ என்று நினைத்தனர்.
questions.explanation: They initially expected a guest to arrive at the door.

questions.question: A 'Merchant' is someone who ______. / 'Merchant' என்பவர் யார்?
questions.explanation: A merchant is a person involved in wholesale trade.

questions.question: Chilies became very popular in ______. / மிளகாய் எங்கு மிகவும் பிரபலமானது?
questions.explanation: Chilies became so fundamental to Indian cuisine that many think they are native.

questions.question: Potatoes reached India via ______. / உருளைக்கிழங்கு இந்தியாவிற்கு எதன் மூலம் வந்தது?
questions.explanation: They were brought by sea traders from far-off lands.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'Native'? / 'Native' என்பதன் எதிர்ச்சொல் என்ன?
questions.explanation: Foreign refers to something from a different country, not native.

questions.question: Chilies were used in food by ______. / மிளகாயை முதலில் உணவில் பயன்படுத்தியவர்கள் ______.
questions.explanation: Aztecs and other Native Americans used chilies long before the rest of the world.

questions.question: Who reached India in 1498? / 1498-இல் இந்தியாவிற்கு வந்தவர் யார்?
questions.explanation: Vasco da Gama discovered the sea route to India in 1498.

questions.question: What did the Portuguese find in Calicut? / கள்ளிக்கோட்டையில் போர்ச்சுகீசியர்கள் எதனைக் கண்டார்கள்?
questions.explanation: They found a bounty of the prestigious black pepper.

questions.question: The story teaches us about food ______. / இந்தக் கதை எதனைப் பற்றி நமக்குக் கற்பிக்கிறது?
questions.explanation: It's about where our 'native' foods actually come from.

questions.question: Which part of the chili contains the most heat? / மிளகாயின் எந்தப் பகுதி அதிக காரத்தன்மை கொண்டது?
questions.explanation: The seeds and the white membrane inside hold most of the capsaicin (heat).

questions.question: Chilies are grown ______ in India now. / இந்தியாவில் மிளகாய் இப்போது ______ வளர்க்கப்படுகிறது.
questions.explanation: Chilies adapt well and are now grown across almost all Indian states.

questions.question: A person who travels on water is a ______. / தண்ணீரில் பயணம் செய்பவர் ______.
questions.explanation: A sailor navigates ships on the sea.

questions.question: Before chilies, Indians used ______ for spice. / மிளகாய் வருவதற்கு முன்பு இந்தியர்கள் காரத்திற்கு எதனைப் பயன்படுத்தினர்?
questions.explanation: Pepper was the primary spice for heat in ancient Indian cooking.

questions.question: The 'Visitor' refers to the ______ journey of vegetables.
questions.explanation: It refers to the long history and distance vegetables traveled to reach us.

questions.question: Mani and Selvi are ______ characters in the story.
questions.explanation: They are the curious children in the narrative.
